topic: Game content extraction blind-box content refactor
recommended_workflow: Start with a focused content rewrite task before code changes.

scope_items.title: Define 20 target blind-box categories
scope_items.description: Use the accepted 20 scene categories and keep the naming short and intuitive.
scope_items.deliverable: Category table with scene hints and optional legacy mapping.

scope_items.title: Create five-layer item pool schema
scope_items.description: Author core_items, support_items, visible_small_items, conditional_items, and blocked_or_risky for each category.

scope_items.title: Rewrite three pilot categories
scope_items.description: Pilot categories are desk-study, underwater-diving, and park-picnic.
scope_items.deliverable: Pilot item lists that map back to large medium small hanging.

scope_items.title: Compatibility mapping
scope_items.description: Map the new model back to the current runtime buckets while UI and extraction logic stay unchanged.

scope_items.title: Validation pass
scope_items.description: Review pilot outputs against visibility, placement, category fit, and risk rules.
scope_items.deliverable: Quality review summary and adjustment notes.

quality_rules: Core items must be immediately identifiable and easy to circle.
Small items must be grouped, blocky, or clearly supported.
Conditional items must depend on explicit support or hanging relations.
Risky items such as lights, mirrors, transparent reflective objects, fine strings, tassels, micro traces, and animal bodies should not be in default pools.
Do not fill categories with repeated container suffixes.
